name: Eligibility Quiz
purpose: Determine whether the principal applicant appears eligible to proceed
Detect cases outside safe self-service scope
Route user to the correct path
Collect only information necessary at eligibility stage

questions.question: What is your citizenship status?
options: I am a dual citizen and one of my citizenships is Canadian
I am a permanent resident of Canada, but not a Canadian citizen
I am not a Canadian citizen

questions.question: Do you currently hold a valid Canadian passport?
Are you currently in Canada or in the United States?
options: I am currently in the United States on valid status
I am currently in the United States and I am not sure whether my status is valid
I am currently in the United States without valid status

questions.question: Have you already invested funds into the U.S. business, or are you in the process of committing funds to it?
options: Yes — funds have already been invested
Yes — funds are in the process of being committed
No — funds are available, but nothing has been committed yet
No — I do not currently have funds available for the investment

questions.question: Approximately how much capital do you expect to invest in the business?
questions.currency_conversion_note: Convert CAD to USD using daily cached rate before applying thresholds. Display rate used to user.

questions.question: Can you document where your investment funds came from and how they moved into the business?
options: Yes — I can document the source and movement of funds
Mostly — I have most of the documentation, but there may be gaps
No — I cannot clearly document the source or movement of funds

questions.question: Are any of your investment funds coming from a loan?
options: Yes — a loan secured only by my personal assets
Yes — a loan secured by the assets of the business I am investing in
Yes — but I am not sure how the loan is secured

questions.question: What best describes your intended role in the business?
options: I will actively develop and direct the business myself
I will actively run the business with one equal business partner
I will mainly be a passive investor

questions.question: Is your business arrangement one of the following?
Is the business primarily any of the following?
options: Passive real estate holding or rental-only investment
A business where I would not be actively involved in management

questions.question: Have you ever been refused a U.S. visa or had a U.S. visa application denied?
options: Yes — one refusal, more than 5 years ago
Yes — one refusal, within the last 5 years

warnings.W-11-OLD: A single refusal more than five years ago carries less weight than a recent refusal, but it must still be disclosed accurately on your DS-160 and addressed in your application. The Interview Simulator will prepare you for the question the officer may ask about it.
warnings.W-11-RECENT: A visa refusal within the last five years will be actively reviewed by the consular officer handling your E-2 application. It must be disclosed on your DS-160. Your cover letter should address it proactively — explaining what has changed since the refusal. Legal review is recommended before proceeding.
warnings.W-11-MULTIPLE: Multiple prior refusals significantly increase the scrutiny your application will receive. This does not automatically disqualify your case, but it requires careful, proactive handling — including a direct explanation in the cover letter. Legal review is strongly recommended.

questions.question: Have you ever been refused entry to the United States, removed, or deported?
Have you ever been convicted of, cautioned for, or charged with a criminal offence in any country — including charges that were dropped, withdrawn, or for which you received a pardon?
options: Yes — a minor conviction, more than 10 years ago
Yes — a minor conviction within the last 10 years

warnings.W-13-OLD: A minor, older conviction may fall within the petty offence exception under U.S. immigration law — meaning it may not constitute a ground of inadmissibility. This depends on the specific offence, the maximum possible sentence, and the actual sentence served. It must still be disclosed accurately on your DS-160. Legal review is recommended.
warnings.W-13-RECENT: A conviction within the last 10 years — even a minor one — will be reviewed by the consular officer. Whether it triggers inadmissibility depends on the offence type and sentence. Do not assume it is resolved without individualized legal review.
warnings.W-13-SERIOUS: A serious conviction requires individualized legal analysis before relying on a self-service preparation tool. Depending on the offence, mandatory inadmissibility grounds may apply. Legal review is essential before proceeding.
warnings.W-13-UNSURE: If you are not sure whether you have a relevant criminal history, clarify this before proceeding. Failing to disclose a conviction on the DS-160 is itself a serious issue. Legal review recommended.

questions.question: Do you own real estate in Canada that you intend to keep while living in the United States?
options: Yes — I own Canadian property and will keep it
Yes — I own Canadian property but may sell it before moving
No — I do not own Canadian real estate

questions.question: Do you have immediate family members — spouse, children, or parents — who will remain living in Canada while you are in the United States?
options: Yes — close family will remain in Canada
No — my immediate family will come with me
Mixed — some family in Canada, some coming with me
I do not have immediate family

questions.question: Will you maintain active Canadian financial accounts, pension plans, or retirement savings (such as an RRSP) while in the United States?
options: Yes — I will keep active Canadian financial accounts
Partially — I will keep some accounts active
No — I plan to close or transfer all accounts

questions.question: Is your business partner a Canadian citizen?
Will your partner also be actively developing and directing the business?
Will any family members apply as your dependents?
If a spouse is applying with you, are you legally married?
Will every dependent applying with you have a valid passport for visa processing?
Do any children applying with you fall into either of these categories: 21 or older, or married?
Does any dependent have prior U.S. visa refusals, entry refusals, removal history, or criminal history?
Where should we send your eligibility result and next-step summary?
If your case includes one or more risk flags, do you understand that E2Pathway is a preparation tool and not a legal service?

hard_stops.PR-01: Principal applicant not a Canadian citizen
hard_stops.PR-02: Principal applicant in U.S. without valid status

attorney_recommended_flags.W-16: Relationship may not qualify for spouse-dependent workflow

non_immigrant_intent_composite.description: After Q0-NI-01 through Q0-NI-03, count confirmed ties

investment_thresholds_usd.note: No statutory minimum. Thresholds are advisory only based on observed practice.

version_notes: Section 0.9 (Non-Immigrant Intent) moved to appear after Section 0.5 (Risk) and before Section 0.6 (Partner Screening)
Q0-11 revised to 4 options: no refusal / one >5yr / one <5yr / multiple
Q0-13 revised to 5 options: none / minor >10yr / minor <10yr / serious / unsure
Warning tiers written in V2.1 style for all new options
